Very simple. Very easy. Ard. very elTlctive...
As far as business goes, we covered this in last month's issue. But, lor new
subscribers, here is a recap of how to get people to buy by getting them to tell you no:
The Best Way To Get AYes,Is To Get Them TellingYou No
This ÍLay sound contrarian (again) but the goal i4 aU my seling is never to get a
yes. It's to get a "no" i¡rstead. This could be an entire newsletter issue in itself. But let
me give you an example. Vühen my ex-copywriting apprentice and I brcke up last
surnñer I was back on the chase again prétty quickly. And, at the time, I was reading
Jim Camp's book a¡out trying to get people to say no first. So,I decided to test it out
on some chicls I lvanted to date. I wou.Id litera-Ily try to get thém to tell me no to dates,
hanging out, etc. Did it work? Ridicriously well. I ¡eme¡n.ber telling one gi¡t who was
being kind ofwishy-washy, "how about we iust be ftiends, r¡./hat do you thi¡*? Thérc
a.re some other girls I arn talking to who a¡e showing a lot more interest."Yes, my ütUe,
fledgirg it worked. But I didn't do it tacticaly. I réally did wa¡t her to say no to dating
me and to just bé friends so I could ftee up ñy mind from wasting time thi¡king about
it. Of course, she changed íe! túne fasf. Bul, il would have been perfectly okay if she
hadn't. I wanted the no- By doing so you eliminate a.ll neediness. There is simply no
need in your voice or words. It's just thé opposite. And it gets everyone talking
honestly and frankly.It lets you draw the Euth out of people - whether it's fig]lring

'I. III,] EI'IAIL PLAYERS NIi\\,SLETTER
were Superman.' But bitch, the o¡ly way I coüld be with you was to NOT be Supe¡¡nan.
Now you realized you made a mistale. Now you walking through the snow, nov¡ you're
walking through A¡rtarctica, trying to find your green crystal, nigga! ftying to look for
the Fortsess of Solitude, because you realüed you're a fucking idiot, because you gave
some bitch all your por¡.rers! And that's what we do! l¡Ve give a bitch aII our powers and
they gét beat up in a diner by some motherfuckel"
Ah yes, nobody has a way with erplaining things, bringing topics down to dre
street level, üe lizard-brain level, lüe Patrice O'Neal. It's a weird paradox but women
are nothing but paradoxical to us dudes.
A¡d what of business? How does this help you with that? The answer is simple:
Don't let a client (or customer) be your only client and last client. Don't forsa-Le othe¡
opportunities because of any ONE client. That one client can leave and dump you at ar-ry
time (and this happens so often it's almost cliche especially in the freelance copy,.writing
world). I have knorne.r way too marry freelancers who got a gig wiü a big client. The cJient
paid a lot. They even needed to bdng in some other writers to help. They made a lot of
monq¡ had plenty of work coming in, and got lazy ald complacent - not marketing
üemselves, not at least even keeping a waiting list on their site for potential clients (which
that alone is pretty powerfirl psychologically to someone interested in hiring you). And
maybe everything goes well for a while but evertually the client starts taking drem for
granted. Starts playing games wiü palaaents or demallding more thal what was agree
do. Starts ta-lking to other writers and compa¡ies on tlre sneak. Then, suddenly, out of the
blue, that big client dumps that cop)rl,1iter All üe income dries up instar.rdy All the help
üey hired are now screwed. They maybe even got themselves into a lot of debt üinking
üe fat times would never end. They never bothered creating their own product line.
Never took side clients on. Never looked out for thernselves. No, üey gave up their super
powers. They became mortal again. They don't have the same network of people wanting
to hire them, they have to build that up a.ll oveq by trudging tllrough the snow looking for
their fortress of solitude like Superman. And, even üen, the oüer clients a-re secretly
wondering "if they're so good, why're üey desperate to be back in the game with us..."
So the message is clear:
Never give a girl or a client your superpowers. I'm not saying to not be faithfi¡l to a
girl or never get married. I'm just saying keep your powers. Don't stop being üe man üey
lell in Iove üü just because they are starting to complain about whatever it is they are
complaining about. In fact, remember ye the Dalte Nero quote I posted in last year's

Always Be Taking Applications
A few monüs ago, I was dating a girl who was getting very aggressive about me
giving her my commitment. I did¡r't blame he¡. We had been dating for months. Like
most girls, she wanted some kind of commitment. Buq elBenbo does not allow himself to

other people, too. But she didn't want to see these other people, she only wanted to see

for example, I'm still alwals taJring applications from odre¡ would-be assista.nts. For one, if

competitor... or il shejust ups and leaves without even giving her two week notice, I have
to have someone I can pull out of the driDl< at a moment's notice. Sometimes you have to
break üe glass on the fire bell arrd pull it. She understood where I was coming from well
enough. She understood her position was my tltet:¡] right now. She was not yet a paid

filI that position. But at that time all I wanted was interns - lots of drem. Eventually, I

and be determined to work ñr me. A¡d üat's üe way you have to approach 21st century
relationships in my humble @ut accurate) opinion. Even if you do have a great girl, wife,
